title: Add a same-lap race-context ranker with race-lap queries
summary: Pit calls are competitive decisions: teams react to rivals, traffic, and undercut windows.
rationale: Pit calls are competitive decisions: teams react to rivals, traffic, and undercut windows. Learning-to-rank over all cars on the same Race-Year-Lap can model who is most likely to pit next even if absolute probabilities are noisy.
implementation_hint: Create a separate LightGBM ranking model with query groups = Race-Year-Lap and relevance = PitNextLap. Feed relative features such as within-lap percentile of TyreLife, LapTime_Delta, position, stint age, and gaps to race-lap medians; blend OOF ranks with the main classifier.
expected_effect: Should supply complementary ordering signal in crowded strategic windows where several drivers have similar absolute degradation but only one or two are true next-lap pit candidates.
risk: Ranking loss optimizes within-query order, not global AUC, so it should be a specialist in an ensemble, not the only model.
